title: armeria alpina subsp. bubanii
intro: Armeria alpina subsp. bubanii, also known as the alpine sea pink, is a plant species belonging to the family Plumbaginaceae. It is a flowering perennial native to the high mountain regions of Central Asia and Europe, and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its antiseptic and expectorant properties, which help to promote healing and relieve congestion. Armeria alpina subsp. bubanii can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to soothe coughs, calm digestive issues, and support overall well-being.
